goal: wave13-v2-community: Ship SITE_DESIGN_v2 Wave 13 (Phase 3 community types + Agent API parity) per docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md. Wave 13 builds on the Wave 12 unified object store + visibility (merged to main, PR #6). Deliver: (1) the Phase 1 community types (discussion, help_request, event, community_announcement) fully wired end to end — human UI on .org plus the same write/action contracts callable by an external agent via Bearer token; (2) Ask Submit / Find Help on .org producing real, community-visible objects through the SAME backend endpoints an external agent uses (unified write API), not local/private stubs; (3) Phase 2 community types (question, mentorship_request, project_request, collaboration_offer, apprenticeship_opportunity) reserved as API stubs plus a matching stub, with their action verbs (incl. Match) surfaced as clearly-labeled reserved affordances. Visibility enforcement and the object store already exist from Wave 12 — extend, do not rebuild. English-only UI, light theme default + dark toggle, content-first editorial per project skills.
summary: Jacky's bot: shipping SITE_DESIGN_v2 Wave 13 — .org community Phase 1 types fully wired (human UI + Agent API) + Ask Submit/Find Help write parity + Phase 2 stubs
acceptanceCriteria: Backend builds + `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` passes on the integrated branch; both web apps build

Phase 1 community types (discussion, help_request, event, community_announcement) have working actions on .org: Reply, Save, Follow, Offer help, Join — with visibility enforced (public for guests, members-only for .org members, private for owner)

The on-site Ask Submit and Find Help flows create real community-visible objects via the SAME contracts an external agent uses (session cookie OR Bearer token hit identical endpoints) — no private/local-only dead-ends
Phase 2 community types are reserved API stubs (accepted-but-flagged) with a matching stub; their action verbs incl. Match render as clearly-labeled reserved affordances in the UI
Agentic Access API docs (/for-agents) on both sites document the community write endpoints + Phase 2 reserved types and parity with on-site Ask
One integrated draft PR to main; docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md updated to mark Wave 13 shipped

tasks.name: shared-foundation
tasks.scopedGoal: Add the Wave 13 shared contracts for community Phase 1 action parity + Phase 2 reserved stubs to @ai-transformation/shared (packages/shared only). BUILD ON TOP of the existing Wave 12 contracts in packages/shared/src/wave12-objects.ts and the existing createApiClient in packages/shared/src/index.ts — do NOT duplicate the object/contribution/visibility/personal schemas that already exist there (knowledgeObjectTypeSchema, communityPhase1ObjectTypeSchema, communityPhase2ReservedObjectTypeSchema, objectRecordSchema, contribution*, commentSchema/commentCreateRequestSchema with a discriminated personalTargetSchema that already supports {targetType:'object'}, bookmarks/notes, moderation, publishPreference, profile, and the existing client namespaces objects/contributions/moderation/publishPreference/notes/annotations/comments/recentlyViewed plus the v1.* Bearer parity namespace). Add a NEW module (e.g. packages/shared/src/wave13-community.ts) re-exported from index.ts that adds: (1) A community ACTION taxonomy: a typed map from each Phase 1 community type to its human action verbs and a separate map for Phase 2 reserved types, plus a helper getCommunityActions(type) and isCommunityPhase2ReservedType(type). Use the spec verbs (discussion: reply, follow, save, turn_into_field_note, draft_reply; help_request: offer_help, save, draft_via_ask; event: join, save, follow; community_announcement: read, save; Phase 2 reserved verbs incl. match, apply, request_mentor, ask_for_intro). Keep verbs as stable string-literal unions. (2) A generic community INTERACTION record + request/response schemas covering follow/unfollow, offer_help, join (and leave/unjoin where natural): fields like id, objectId, userId (nullable), site, kind ('follow'|'offer_help'|'join'), body (optional, max 8000), createdAt/updatedAt; plus list response and a delete/undo response. REPLY should reuse the existing public commentSchema with target {targetType:'object', targetId:<objectId>} — add only a thin community-reply request wrapper if helpful, do not fork the comment model. (3) Phase 2 reserved-type stub schemas: a matchRequest/matchResponse (reserved/no-op result shape with a reserved boolean + message) and a reservedTypeResult shape backend can return when a reserved community type is created. (4) Extend createApiClient with a NEW `community` namespace on BOTH the session client and the existing `v1` (Bearer) namespace, using clean REST-ish paths under /api/community/* and /api/v1/community/* (e.g. listByType, getWithReplies, reply, follow, unfollow, offerHelp, join, leave, listInteractions, matchStub). The session and v1 method shapes MUST be identical so on-site Ask and external agents share contracts. Reuse existing helpers (requestSessionJson/withJsonBody/buildQuery and the v1 Bearer equivalents) — match their style exactly. Run `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck` (or tsc --noEmit in packages/shared) and ensure it passes. Do NOT touch apps/**, packages/content/**, packages/backend, data/**, or docs/**.
tasks.brief: Read docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md (§4 Ask modes incl. the unified-API implementation note, §5 object model + §5.3 community Phase 1/Phase 2 types + action verbs, §6 contextual actions, §11 Phase 3/Wave 13). Wave 12 already shipped the unified object store, visibility, contributions, personal layer (bookmarks/notes/annotations/comments/recently-viewed), moderation, and a typed API client with a v1 Bearer parity namespace — your job is the THIN community-action + Phase 2-stub layer on top, keeping the session and Bearer contracts identical. This is the dependency BOTH the backend and the .org UI worker branch from, so keep exports clean, generic, and well-typed. No backend logic, no UI — schemas + client only. English code/docs.
acceptance: A new community module is re-exported from @ai-transformation/shared without duplicating Wave 12 object/contribution/visibility/personal schemas
Exports a community action-verb taxonomy for Phase 1 types + a reserved taxonomy for Phase 2 types, with getCommunityActions(type) and isCommunityPhase2ReservedType(type) helpers
Exports a generic community interaction record + create/list/delete request/response schemas for follow/offer_help/join; reply reuses the existing public comment contract (object target)
Exports Phase 2 reserved matching stub schemas (matchRequest/matchResponse) + a reserved-type result shape
createApiClient gains an identical `community` namespace on BOTH the session client and the v1 Bearer namespace (paths /api/community/* and /api/v1/community/*)
packages/shared typecheck passes; no files outside packages/shared changed

tasks.name: backend-community
tasks.scopedGoal: Implement the Wave 13 community Phase 1 action endpoints + Agent API parity in apps/backend, matching EXACTLY the shared `community` contract added by shared-foundation (session paths /api/community/* and Bearer paths /api/v1/community/*; identical shapes). Build on the existing objects lane and DB helpers — do NOT rebuild the object store or visibility. Existing infra you must reuse: apps/backend/src/lanes/objects/index.ts (mounted on both /api and /api/v1), apps/backend/src/db/objects.ts (createObject/listObjectsForRequester/getObjectByIdForRequester/submitObject + the single authoritative buildVisibilityFilter + VisibilityQueryContext), apps/backend/src/db/personal.ts (createComment/listComments etc.), the resolveRequester/toVisibilityContext/requireAuthenticated/requireResolvedUser helpers and the Bearer (verifyWriteToken + getUserByEmail) path already in the objects lane. Deliver: (1) Community READ: listByType (community objects filtered by type, visibility-enforced via buildVisibilityFilter — public for guests, members-only for that-site members, private for owner) and getWithReplies (object + its public comments/replies). (2) Phase 1 ACTIONS: reply = create a public comment targeting the community object (reuse the existing comment create path with {targetType:'object'}); follow/unfollow = a new idempotent follows table (one row per user+object); offer_help and join/leave = a new community_interactions table (idempotent where natural, kind in 'offer_help'|'join'). Add idempotent migrations in the Wave 12 style (PRAGMA table_info / CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS in db/objects.ts or a new db/community.ts). (3) ASK SUBMIT / FIND HELP PARITY: ensure Find Help creates a community help_request that, after submit/auto-publish per the user's publish preference, becomes visible in Community (status published/members-or-public per visibility) rather than a dead private draft; Submit creates a knowledge contribution through the same moderation/publish path. CRITICAL: every community write + the help_request/contribution create must be callable IDENTICALLY via session cookie (/api) and Bearer token (/api/v1) — the SAME endpoints/handlers an external agent uses. Mount the new community router on BOTH /api and /api/v1, mirroring how objectsRouter is mounted. (4) PHASE 2 reserved types: accept create/list for reserved community types but mark them reserved (store flagged, e.g. status 'draft' + metadata.reserved=true, OR return the shared reserved-type result) and add a matching stub endpoint returning the shared reserved/no-op matchResponse. No real matching logic. (5) TESTS: add a community.test.ts (or extend objects.test.ts) covering the visibility matrix for community listByType/getWithReplies (anonymous / io-member / org-member / owner / bearer-owner), reply+follow+offer_help+join happy paths, agent-parity (Bearer token) for help_request create AND a community action, and Phase 2 reserved handling. Run `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck` and ensure both pass (run pnpm install first if node_modules is missing). Do NOT touch apps/web-io/**, apps/web-org/**, apps/combined/**, packages/**, or data/**.
tasks.brief: Read docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §4 (unified-API note: on-site Ask should call the same backend contracts as the Agentic Access API), §5 (visibility + community Phase 1/Phase 2 types), §7 (moderation/publish: auto-publish vs review per user/submission, never elevates to public without author choice), §11 (Phase 3/Wave 13). The shared-foundation branch you start from defines the exact community client contract (paths + shapes) — implement handlers that satisfy it on BOTH /api and /api/v1. Visibility is ALREADY centralized in buildVisibilityFilter; route every community read through a VisibilityQueryContext so private/members-only never leak. The Bearer-owner mapping (token email -> users row via getUserByEmail) already exists in resolveRequester — reuse it so an agent's writes are owned by the same account as the human. Keep migrations idempotent and additive (Wave 12 used PRAGMA table_info guards). English code. Note: Wave 12's backend-objects worker reported needing `pnpm install` because node_modules were missing — run it first.
acceptance: New community router implements listByType + getWithReplies with visibility enforced via buildVisibilityFilter, mounted on BOTH /api and /api/v1
Phase 1 actions work: reply (public comment on object), follow/unfollow (idempotent follows table), offer_help and join/leave (community_interactions table); migrations are idempotent + additive
Find Help creates a community help_request that becomes visible after submit/auto-publish per publish preference; Submit creates a knowledge contribution through the moderation/publish path
Every community write + help_request/contribution create is callable identically via session cookie and Bearer token (same handlers on /api and /api/v1)
Phase 2 reserved community types are accepted-but-flagged and a matching stub endpoint returns the shared reserved/no-op result
community tests cover visibility matrix + Phase 1 actions + Bearer agent-parity + Phase 2 reserved; `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` and typecheck pass
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- From repo root run `pnpm install` if node_modules missing.
## Automated
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck` must pass.
- Confirm the community router is mounted on BOTH /api and /api/v1 (grep the index/lane mount).
## Manual
- Exercise: create a help_request via Find Help path under session AND via Bearer token; confirm identical result + that an auto-published one appears in community listByType; confirm members-only/private items are filtered for anonymous/cross-site requesters; confirm reply/follow/offer_help/join persist and are idempotent where designed; confirm a Phase 2 reserved type create is flagged and the matching stub returns a reserved result.
## Gotchas
- Reuse buildVisibilityFilter (do not write a second visibility path). Bearer-owner maps to the same users row as the human via getUserByEmail. Auto-publish must respect visibility (never silently public).

tasks.name: web-org-community
tasks.scopedGoal: Wire the ai-transformation.org Community Phase 1 types fully (human UI) and document Agent API parity, consuming the shared `community` client methods added by shared-foundation (do NOT invent shadow fetch shapes; use getApiClient().community.* and the existing objects/notes/contributions/bookmarks clients). You start from the shared-foundation branch. Deliver in apps/web-org (+ data/curated/org-*.json): (1) A community DETAIL route app/community/[id] that renders the object + its replies and the working action verbs PER TYPE via the shared client: Reply (post a public comment), Save (bookmark — already wired via components/save-button.tsx + lib/use-bookmarks.ts), Follow/Unfollow, Offer help (for help_request), Join/Leave (for event). Members get full actions; guests get public read + a sign-in prompt; for Phase 2 RESERVED community types, render their action verbs (incl. Match) as clearly-labeled 'Reserved / coming soon' affordances (non-functional), using isCommunityPhase2ReservedType + the reserved action taxonomy from shared. (2) Update components/community-highlights.tsx: cards link to /community/[id]; replace the disabled 'Available with community types (Wave 13)' placeholder verbs with the real wired actions (Save/Follow + a Reply/Open link to detail; Offer help / Join where the type applies). Keep the sample-card fallback when there are no live objects. (3) Update components/ask-modes.tsx so Find Help creates a community-visible help_request (save draft then submit, or a single create, per the shared contract — auto/review honoring the user's publish preference) and Submit creates a contribution through the unified endpoint that an external agent would also use; Capture stays a private note. Remove the now-stale 'arrives in Wave 13' / 'Full reply and matching actions arrive in Wave 13' copy and reflect that these actions are live (Phase 2 matching remains 'coming'). Keep the guest localStorage fallback for unauthenticated users. (4) Update the .org /for-agents page (apps/web-org/app/for-agents/page.tsx) to document the community write + action endpoints and Phase 2 reserved types available via the Agentic Access API (Bearer token, /api/v1/community/* and /api/v1/contributions), noting parity with the on-site Ask Submit/Find Help. (5) Update data/curated/org-home.json + data/curated/org-hub-index.json CTAs/copy if any reference Wave-13-pending community actions. Ensure `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build` (or the repo web-org build) succeeds. Do NOT touch apps/web-io/**, packages/**, apps/backend/**, apps/combined/**.
tasks.brief: READ FIRST: docs/SITE_DESIGN_v2.md §2 (.org positioning), §3.3 (.org route map — /community public-only for guests vs full opportunity layer for members), §4 (Ask modes + unified-API note), §5.3 (community Phase 1 types + action verbs + Phase 2 reserved), §6 (contextual Ask prefill: Draft reply, Turn discussion into field note, Submit via Agent), §11 (Phase 3/Wave 13). LOAD the project UI skills before building UI: .agents/skills/editorial-ui/SKILL.md and .agents/skills/ux-copy/SKILL.md (they override generic frontend skills — content-first editorial, English-only UI, light default + dark toggle). Existing wiring to reuse: components/community-highlights.tsx already lists live published community objects and falls back to lib/community-highlights.ts samples; lib/object-display.ts has COMMUNITY_TYPE_LABEL/COMMUNITY_TYPE_VERBS/objectTarget/objectTitle; lib/ask-prefill.ts builds contextual /ask links; lib/use-bookmarks.ts + components/save-button.tsx implement Save; components/ask-modes.tsx currently saves Find Help as a private draft and Submit as a field_note draft — upgrade these to community-visible writes via the shared client. lib/use-auth-user.ts gives audience guest/member. You depend ONLY on shared-foundation; code against the typed shared client contract (the backend worker implements it in parallel) — your build/typecheck must pass against the client interface even though the live backend is integrated later. NO legacy redirects. Capture before/after screenshots or a short screen recording of the community detail + actions and Ask Find Help/Submit, and reference the artifact path in your handoff.
acceptance: app/community/[id] renders a community object + replies with working per-type actions via the shared client: Reply, Save, Follow/Unfollow, Offer help (help_request), Join/Leave (event)
Phase 2 reserved community types render their verbs (incl. Match) as clearly-labeled reserved/coming-soon affordances using the shared reserved taxonomy
community-highlights cards link to /community/[id] and expose the real wired actions instead of disabled Wave-13 placeholders (sample fallback retained when empty)

Ask Find Help creates a community-visible help_request and Submit creates a contribution via the same unified contracts an external agent uses; Capture stays a private note; guest localStorage fallback retained
.org /for-agents documents the community write/action endpoints + Phase 2 reserved types (Bearer /api/v1) and parity with on-site Ask
data/curated/org-*.json CTAs updated where they referenced Wave-13-pending actions; web-org builds
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- Build web-org with the repo build (e.g. `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build`).
## Automated
- Build + type-check must pass. Confirm app/community/[id] route exists and uses getApiClient().community.* (no shadow fetch).
## Manual
- Run web-org dev: open /community, click a card to /community/[id], exercise Reply/Save/Follow/Offer help/Join (member) and confirm guest sees public read + sign-in prompt; open /ask, run Find Help and Submit and confirm they create community-visible/contribution items (not private dead-ends); confirm Phase 2 reserved verbs show as reserved; confirm /for-agents documents the agent parity.
## Gotchas
- Use the shared client only; the backend is integrated later so build against the contract. English-only UI, light default + dark toggle.

tasks.name: integrate-wave13
tasks.scopedGoal: Produce the single integrated Wave 13 deliverable branch and open ONE draft PR to main. You start from the backend-community branch (which already includes the shared-foundation commits as common ancestor). Merge `orch/wave13-v2-community/web-org-community` into the current branch. Expected conflicts are minimal because backend-community touches apps/backend/** and web-org-community touches apps/web-org/** + data/curated/org-*.json (disjoint), with packages/shared as identical common ancestor; if conflicts arise in packages/shared or docs, keep BOTH the backend handlers and the .org UI working against the shared community contract (do not drop either side's needs). After merging: run `pnpm install`, then `pnpm turbo build` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` from repo root; fix any integration-only breakage (import drift, type mismatches between the backend and .org use of the shared community client). Then, as a LIGHT doc-only change, update apps/web-io/app/for-agents/page.tsx to note that the community write/action endpoints + Phase 2 reserved types are available via the shared Agentic Access API (/api/v1, Bearer) — keep it brief since .io has no community UI. Update docs to reflect Wave 13 shipped: docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md and docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md (note: community Phase 1 types fully wired human UI + Agent API on .org, Ask Submit/Find Help now hit the unified write API with session/Bearer parity, Phase 2 community types reserved as API stubs + matching stub, visibility enforced via Wave 12 buildVisibilityFilter; Wave 14 = personalization v2 + Phase 2 community types + matching experiments next). Commit, push exactly your branch, and open a DRAFT PR to main titled 'Wave 13: SITE_DESIGN_v2 Phase 3 community types + Agent API parity' summarizing the backend endpoints, the .org community UI, the Ask write-parity, and the Phase 2 stubs. Do NOT merge the PR or mark it ready.
tasks.brief: This is a merge + integration + docs task (planners do not code, so this worker performs the merge). The backend and .org branches are disjoint by directory; the main risk is divergent use of the shared community client contract — reconcile at the type level so `pnpm turbo build` passes for backend + both web apps and backend tests stay green. Read the upstream handoffs (pasted into your prompt) to learn each branch's actual deliverable branch and any deviations. Keep the merge history with both parents. English docs/code per project-guidelines. The .io /for-agents edit is documentation only (no community UI on .io). After build+test green, update docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md, then open the draft PR via the ManagePullRequest flow against main. Note: prior waves found node_modules missing — run `pnpm install` first.
acceptance: web-org-community branch merged into the deliverable branch with both parents in history
pnpm turbo build passes for backend + web-io + web-org
pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test passes (incl. the new community tests)
.io /for-agents notes community write/action endpoints available via /api/v1 Bearer (light doc-only)
docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md updated to reflect Wave 13 shipped
One DRAFT PR opened to main (not merged, not marked ready) summarizing community types, Agent API parity, Ask write-parity, and Phase 2 stubs
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `pnpm install` on the integrated branch.
## Automated
- `pnpm turbo build` (backend + both web apps) and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` must pass.
## Manual
- Spot-check that .org community detail + actions and Ask Find Help/Submit wire to the shared community client, and that the backend community router is mounted on /api and /api/v1.
## Gotchas
- Preserve both backend and .org use of the shared community contract; the .io for-agents change is doc-only.

tasks.name: verify-wave13
tasks.scopedGoal: Verify the integrated Wave 13 branch against the planner acceptance criteria and SITE_DESIGN_v2.md Phase 3. Run `pnpm install`, `pnpm turbo build` (backend + both web apps), and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`. Confirm with execution evidence: (a) the community router is mounted on BOTH /api and /api/v1 and the new community tests pass (visibility matrix + Phase 1 actions + Bearer agent-parity + Phase 2 reserved); (b) Phase 1 community types (discussion, help_request, event, community_announcement) have working Reply/Save/Follow/Offer help/Join wired on .org via the shared client (inspect app/community/[id] + community-highlights.tsx — confirm no shadow fetch, real getApiClient().community.* usage); (c) the on-site Ask Submit and Find Help paths call the SAME contracts an external agent uses (session and Bearer hit identical endpoints/handlers) and produce community-visible/contribution objects rather than private-only stubs; (d) Phase 2 community types are reserved API stubs (accepted-but-flagged) with a matching stub, and their verbs incl. Match render as clearly-labeled reserved affordances in the UI; (e) /for-agents on both sites documents the community write endpoints + Phase 2 reserved types; (f) docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md mark Wave 13 shipped and a single draft PR exists. Report execution evidence per criterion; do not pattern-match. If the env blocks a live UI check, run the build + backend tests + route/mount/code-inspection checks and mark unit-test-verified or verifier-blocked honestly.
acceptance: Verification section includes execution evidence (build + backend test output + router-mount + code/route inspection) for every integrate-wave13 acceptance criterion and the planner-level community Phase 1 wiring, Ask write-parity, and Phase 2 reserved-stub claims
